task_context.actionable_question: Am I a procrastinator and is it costing me marks?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Use submission_delay_avg [FE]. Identify if late submission is systematic. Link to score.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: It looks like you haven't submitted any assessments yet, which suggests a pattern of procrastination. This could be impacting your scores, as timely submissions are crucial for success.

Insights: Lack of Submissions: You have not submitted any assessments, which indicates a potential habit of delaying your work. This is reflected in your punctuality rate of 0, meaning you haven't submitted on time for any assessments. | Impact on Scores: Your normalized scores for the quizzes and exam are currently 0 and 55, respectively. Without timely submissions, you risk not achieving higher scores.

Educational implications: Timely submissions are essential for maintaining good grades and understanding course material.

Recommendations: high - Set specific deadlines for yourself to complete assessments before the due dates. - Creating personal deadlines can help you manage your time better and reduce procrastination. | medium - Reach out to your instructor for support or clarification on assessments. - Getting help can motivate you to start working on your assignments and improve your engagement.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify whether late submission is systematic.
Describe the observed relationship between submission delay and score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use submission_delay_avg as the primary delay metric; do not infer procrastination from score alone.
Frame the delay-score relationship as correlational, not causal.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
